peripheral_notes.summary: Header pins with documented native USB data functions.

notes: ESP_BOOT in the official Boot button allocation for Boot button.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-up.
Controls boot mode with GPIO46; GPIO0=0 and GPIO46=0 selects joint download boot mode.
USB_DN in the official USB-C allocation for USB-C.
USB_D pins are connected to USB Serial/JTAG by default and need IO MUX reconfiguration before regular GPIO use.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
Power-up high-level glitches occur twice; Espressif lists about 3.2 ms total duration and about 2 ms delay.
USB_DP in the official USB-C allocation for USB-C.
USB_D pins are connected to USB Serial/JTAG by default and need IO MUX reconfiguration before regular GPIO use.
Power-up pull-down glitch for about 60 us.
Power-up high-level glitches occur twice; Espressif lists about 3.2 ms total duration and about 2 ms delay.
POWER_CTRL in the official Power allocation for Power.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
CODEC_PWR_CTRL in the official Audio power allocation for Audio power.
Differential SPI/SUBSPI negative clock.
On ESP32-S3R8V and ESP32-S3R16V, this pin can operate at 1.8 V.
I2C_SCL in the official Shared I2C allocation for Shared I2C.
Shared clock for IMU, touch, battery, audio, and expansion I2C paths.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
I2C_SDA in the official Shared I2C allocation for Shared I2C.
Shared data for IMU, touch, battery, audio, and expansion I2C paths.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_SDA0 in the official LCD QSPI allocation for LCD QSPI.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-down.
Controls boot mode with GPIO0; GPIO0=0 and GPIO46=0 selects joint download boot mode.
Also participates in ROM message printing control.
LCD_SDA1 in the official LCD QSPI allocation for LCD QSPI.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_SDA2 in the official LCD QSPI allocation for LCD QSPI.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_SDA3 in the official LCD QSPI allocation for LCD QSPI.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_SCL in the official LCD QSPI allocation for LCD QSPI.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
Power-up high-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_DC in the official LCD control allocation for LCD control.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-down.
Participates in VDD_SPI voltage selection: default GPIO45=0 selects 3.3 V when eFuse does not force another value.
LCD_BLK in the official LCD control allocation for LCD control.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
LCD_CS in the official LCD control allocation for LCD control.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
LCD_RST_CTRL in the official LCD control allocation for LCD control.
Differential SPI/SUBSPI positive clock.
On ESP32-S3R8V and ESP32-S3R16V, this pin can operate at 1.8 V.
LCD_TE in the official LCD control allocation for LCD control.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
TP_INT in the official Touch allocation for Touch.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
TOUCH_PAD1 in the official Touch allocation for Touch.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
TOUCH_PAD2 in the official Touch allocation for Touch.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
I2S_MCLK in the official Audio I2S allocation for Audio I2S.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
I2S_DI in the official Audio I2S allocation for Audio I2S.
Strapping pin for JTAG signal source selection; default is floating.
Espressif notes GPIO3 has no internal pull resistors and the external circuit must not be high impedance during strapping.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
I2S_WS in the official Audio I2S allocation for Audio I2S.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
I2S_BCK in the official Audio I2S allocation for Audio I2S.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
I2S_DO in the official Audio I2S allocation for Audio I2S.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
PA_CTRL in the official Speaker amplifier allocation for Speaker amplifier.
Package pin name is XTAL_32K_P; GPIO identity is GPIO15.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
SD_DA0 in the official MicroSD allocation for MicroSD.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
SD_CLK in the official MicroSD allocation for MicroSD.
Package pin name is XTAL_32K_N; GPIO identity is GPIO16.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
SD_CMD in the official MicroSD allocation for MicroSD.
IMU_INT1 in the official IMU allocation for IMU.
U1RXD in the official Magnetic connector allocation for Magnetic connector.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
U1TXD in the official Magnetic connector allocation for Magnetic connector.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.

general_warnings: Confirm the exact module variant before using header GPIOs reserved for on-module memory.
Review each listed caution before wiring a board-header GPIO.

power: USB-C and the 3.7 V lithium battery path feed the board power system.
The schematic includes power-control, battery-management, and codec power-control circuits.
programming: USB-C is used for power, programming download, and debugging. GPIO0 is tied to the boot function.
onboard_hardware: Circular LCD, dual microphones, ES7210 audio ADC, ES8311 codec, NS4150B amplifier, BMI270 IMU, MicroSD slot, touch pads, green LED, battery gauge, and magnetic connector.
